summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined Ukraine's development of dual-control FPV drones capable of switching between fiber optic and radio links when the cable snaps, with the Kolibri 13-O model featuring 20 km of fiber optics now codified in Ukraine. The Ukrainian military has tested these systems, which remain online even if the fiber link fails, and engineers are working to adapt FPV drones to destroy helicopters by overcoming rotor turbulence, building on the first Ka-52 downing. Recent footage confirms an FPV drone downed a Russian Ka-52 near Pokrovsk, killing the crew, with Ukraine awarding soldiers involved in the strike. Meanwhile, Sparta UAVs are set to boost Ukraine's FPV drone range, and Ukraine is turning to Middle East partners amid a European cold shoulder. In other developments, a Russian FPV drone struck an electric train in Kharkiv, killing one, and another hit a civilian car in Zolochiv, killing a man.
